metadata.name: Traefik Retry Middleware
metadata.description: Traefik Middleware CRD for retrying failed upstream requests with a configurable attempt count and optional initial interval.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Creates a Traefik Middleware resource for `retry` so transient upstream failures can be retried before the client sees an error.

## Prerequisites
- Traefik must be installed with the Kubernetes CRD provider enabled.
- Confirm the upstream application can safely tolerate retries for the routes where you plan to attach this middleware.

## Apply
- Render `main.yaml`, review the retry count and initial interval, then apply it with `kubectl apply -f main.yaml`.
- Attach the middleware only to idempotent or otherwise retry-safe paths unless you fully understand the application behavior.

## Follow-Up
- Watch access logs and upstream metrics so you can verify the middleware is improving resilience instead of hiding instability.
- Keep retry counts conservative for slow or stateful backends.

## References
- [Retry Middleware](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/middlewares/http/retry/)
- [Traefik Kubernetes CRD](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/routing/providers/kubernetes-crd/)

items.name: resource_name
items.title: Middleware name
items.description: Name to use for the Middleware resource.

items.name: attempts
items.title: Attempts
items.description: Number of times Traefik should retry failed requests.

items.name: initial_interval
items.title: Initial interval
items.description: Initial wait time before the first retry attempt.
